tif

Interface TifMetadataProvider

  • All Superinterfaces:
    java.lang.Iterable<DirectoryIFD>, Metadata<DirectoryIFD>
    All Known Implementing Classes:
    TifMetadata

    public interface TifMetadataProvider
extends Metadata<DirectoryIFD>
    A dedicated interface for accessing and managing TIFF-based metadata structures.

    This contract specialises Metadata to support the segmented architecture of Tagged Image File Format structures. It handles internal structural links across multiple Image File Directories (IFDs), including primary, EXIF, and GPS sub-blocks, while unifying traditional binary tags and modern, XML-based XmpDirectory fragments under a single API footprint.

    Since:
    13 August 2025
    Version:
    1.2
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      void addXmpDirectory(XmpDirectory dir)
      Integrates an XMP metadata directory for unified access.
      void clear()
      Resets the metadata container to an entirely empty state.
      DirectoryIFD getDirectory(DirectoryIdentifier dirKey)
      Retrieves a specific Image File Directory (IFD) by its identifier.
      XmpDirectory getXmpDirectory()
      Retrieves the attached XMP metadata directory.
      void setByteOrder(java.nio.ByteOrder order)
      Sets the specified byte order used to interpret multi-byte raw data correctly.

      • Methods inherited from interface common.Metadata

        addDirectory, extractDate, extractZonedDateTime, getByteOrder, hasExifData, hasMetadata, hasXmpData, isEmpty, removeDirectory

      • Methods inherited from interface java.lang.Iterable

        forEach, iterator, spliterator

    • Method Detail

      • getDirectory

        DirectoryIFD getDirectory(DirectoryIdentifier dirKey)
        Retrieves a specific Image File Directory (IFD) by its identifier.
        Parameters:
        dirKey - the identity of the directory to retrieve, such as IFD0, EXIF_SUBIFD, or GPS
        Returns:
        the matching DirectoryIFD, or null if the directory is not present

      • setByteOrder

        void setByteOrder(java.nio.ByteOrder order)
        Sets the specified byte order used to interpret multi-byte raw data correctly.
        Parameters:
        order - either ByteOrder.BIG_ENDIAN or ByteOrder.LITTLE_ENDIAN

      • clear

        void clear()
        Resets the metadata container to an entirely empty state.

      • addXmpDirectory

        void addXmpDirectory(XmpDirectory dir)
        Integrates an XMP metadata directory for unified access.

        XMP is often embedded in TIFF-based formats within a global tag (ID 0x02BC). This method allows the parsed XMP object to be managed alongside standard IFD tags.

        Parameters:
        dir - the XmpDirectory containing the parsed XML metadata

      • getXmpDirectory

        XmpDirectory getXmpDirectory()
        Retrieves the attached XMP metadata directory.
        Returns:
        the XmpDirectory instance, or null if no XMP data exists
